location.office_time_required: at least three days per week

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in Sales / Field Operations
Founding enterprise sales operations leader role
Strategic partner to Enterprise Sales VP

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience in Sales / Field Operations, ideally within a high-growth SaaS or AI company
Advanced Salesforce expertise: reporting, dashboards, data integrity, user administration, etc.
Exceptional analytical and data modeling skills: expert in Excel/Google Sheets; able to translate complex data into actionable insights
Strong project management skills: able to manage multiple projects from scope to delivery; organized and detail-oriented
Exceptional communication: clear, concise written and verbal skills; adept at presenting insights and recommendations to leadership
Strategic business acumen: able to translate sales strategy into operational plans; proactive, structured problem-solver with good judgment

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with Tableau, Clari, Gong, Outreach, SQL
Experience with working closely with Enterprise Sales Operations

scope.surface: Enterprise Sales Operations — founding role building the enterprise ops function at Intercom, supporting global Enterprise Sales VP and organization
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — AI customer support agent (Fin) sold to enterprise businesses
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational efficiency for enterprise sales team
core_responsibilities: Act as strategic partner to Global Enterprise sales leaders
Develop and refine scalable operational cadences for sales forecasting, quarterly business reviews, and performance reporting
Own and drive cross-functional projects including sales forecasting methodology, books of business, account coverage models, and Salesforce enhancements
Collaborate with stakeholders across Sales, Business Systems, Sales Enablement, Finance, Customer Success, and Revenue Operations
Regularly analyze sales performance data, developing and maintaining dashboards and reporting frameworks
Identify opportunities to leverage AI to enhance processes and automate workflows
Document methods and reasoning to ensure workflows are scalable and repeatable

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($180K–$222K base) plus RSUs and bonus/OTE
Founding enterprise ops role — high visibility and influence opportunity
Strategic partner to Enterprise Sales VP — direct access to senior leadership
Clear mission and product context (Fin AI Customer Agent)
Company at significant scale (~30,000 global customers) with continued growth
Hybrid policy explicitly described (3 days/week in office)
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least three days per week in office; San Francisco Bay Area only — eliminates remote candidates
JD uses both 'Fin' and 'Intercom' interchangeably as company name, which may cause confusion about the legal employer entity
No team size or reporting structure disclosed beyond 'strategic partner to Enterprise Sales VP'

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required + comp range + founding role framing)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false from IC framing — no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred false from hybrid + Bay Area comp range framing)
ambiguities: The JD switches between 'Intercom' and 'Fin' as the company name — the opening describes 'Fin is the AI Customer Agent company' and policies refer to 'Fin' as the employer, but the role is listed under 'Intercom' on Greenhouse. Treated as Intercom (the parent) per identity fields provided.
The role title in the identity fields is 'Senior Sales Strategy & Operations Manager' but the JD body opening calls it 'Senior Sales Operations Manager' — minor inconsistency noted.

The JD says 'ideally within a high-growth SaaS or AI company' for the 7+ years requirement — this could be read as a soft qualifier on an otherwise hard years requirement. Treated as hard (years) with the SaaS/AI context as a soft preference, consistent with the main requirement framing.
